## Root Cause Summary

Affecting NVL as well, seen first on DMR. 

It seems that the resolved core_status_ccp_state is used to decide both on resolved 

wish

 state and resolved 

wish

 sub state that are reported to pCode. 

If one core in the module has a lower state than the other, the reported wish state and wish substate is of that core. 

If both cores have the same value, the wish sub state is taken from PAS policy based on core_status_dec (see image below). 

If both cores are in CC1, the PAS policy decides 

## Raw HSD Text

### Forum Notes
[26ww2.1]

Hector noted that the PMA substate for C1 was unexpectedly set to four instead of the expected values (0 for C1 or 1 for C1E), and planned to verify if this issue matches another previously filed HSD, updating the documentation and merging if appropriate.

### Description
Affecting NVL as well, seen first on DMR. 

It seems that the resolved core_status_ccp_state is used to decide both on resolved 

wish

 state and resolved 

wish

 sub state that are reported to pCode. 

If one core in the module has a lower state than the other, the reported wish state and wish substate is of that core. 

If both cores have the same value, the wish sub state is taken from PAS policy based on core_status_dec (see image below). 

If both cores are in CC1, the PAS policy decides that the wish sub state is 0x4, even though the request can be 0 or 1 (as expected for CC1). 

If there was no demotion the wish sub state is pushed as is (and real use case is asking 0 or 1 as sub states for CC1) causing further mismatch between different states:

### Comments (latest)
++++22611668154 mbfausto
Is this a spec contradiction / ordering needing PM/Core Arch to determine what should happen in this case?  Or you are stating what you are seeing in the RTL and it should NOT be grabbing from the PAS policy when both cores in CC1?     If both cores have the same value, the wish sub state is taken from PAS policy based on core_status_dec (see image below).      If both cores are in CC1, the PAS policy decides that the wish sub state is 0x4, even though the request can be 0 or 1 (as expected for CC1). 
++++1363541960 eladva1
The sub states for C1 are either 0 for a "regular" C1, or 1 for a C1e per legacy behavior. Other states should be clipped to C1 or C1e based on the chip's policy on C1e.  This looks like a missing arch def or a potential mis-communication on the expected substate value between core and SoC on Core's reporting to SoC, as the sub state handling is missing from the HAS.  If the module is in MC1 where one core is in CC6 and one in CC1, the resulting sub state would be taken from the cores' sub state, effectively being 0/1. If both cores are in CC1, the sub state is suddenly 0x4.  No reason it should be different if both cores are in CC1, however @Benor, Ofek  can complete the picture on how to classify this issue. 
++++14614939144 hmpicosm
This issue looks similar to this other HSD https://hsdes.intel.com/appstore/article-one/#/14026586861

### Conclusion
no_root_cause.rejected
